session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8618 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by running the required command to fetch PR feedback:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: git diff --stat -M -C main...HEAD 2>/dev/null || git diff --stat -M -C $(git rev-parse origin/main 2>/dev/null || echo 'main')...HEAD
Get file restructuring context for the current branch

session.tool_result:
{ "success": true, "pr_number": 8618, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8618", "pr_title": "Always run `uv sync` in worktree activation scripts", "review_threads": [ { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5xlc3H", "path": "docs/learned/erk/workspace-activation.md", "line": 49, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — source pointer uses stale line numbers.

While updating this section, note that the HTML comment source pointers at lines 19 and 23 reference `src/erk/cli/activation.py:193` — a stale line number. After this PR's changes, the `uv pip install` command is at line 208.

Risk: High maintenance burden as code evolves. Per `docs/learned/documentation/source-pointers.md`, prefer name-based identifiers over line numbers:

Replace `<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/activation.py:193 -->` with:
`<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/activation.py, render_activation_script -->`

Name-based identifiers survive refactoring; line numbers go stale silently.", "created_at": "2026-03-02T15:57:28Z" } ] } ], "discussion_comments": [ { "id": 3985213291, "author": "schrockn", "body": "Plan Queued for Implementation
